title: frangula alnus subsp. alnus
intro: Frangula alnus subsp. alnus, also known as black alder or green alder, is a subspecies of the Frangula alnus plant, a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anthraquinone compounds that act as a laxative and have anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Frangula alnus subsp. alnus include relief from constipation, diarrhea, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Frangula alnus subsp. alnus is typically prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal purposes, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
